The gospel is a message of grace that we hear. The time of the gospel is a time of salvation; the means of grace are the means of salvation; the offers of the gospel are offers of salvation; and now is the right time to accept these offers. Tomorrow belongs to no one: we do not know what will happen tomorrow, nor where we will be. We enjoy a day of grace now; therefore, let everyone be careful not to neglect it. Ministers of the gospel should see themselves as God’s servants and act in every way that is fitting for that role. The apostle did so by being patient in suffering, acting from good principles, and having proper temperance and behavior. Believers in this world need God's grace to protect them from temptations, so they can have a good reputation among people without pride; and so they can bear their insults with patience. They have nothing within themselves, but possess everything through Christ. A Christian’s life is made up of such differences, and through a variety of circumstances and reports, our way to heaven will be; and we should be careful in all things to approve ourselves to God. The gospel, when faithfully preached and fully received, improves the condition even of the poorest people. They save what they previously wasted, and diligently use their time for useful purposes. They gain by religion, and thus are made rich, both for the world to come and for this one, compared with their sinful state before they received the gospel.

It is wrong for believers to associate with wicked and ungodly people. The word unbeliever refers to all who lack true faith. True pastors will warn their beloved children in the gospel not to be unequally yoked. The harmful effects of ignoring scriptural instructions about marriage are clear. Instead of a suitable partner, the union brings a trap. Those whose cross it is to be united unequally, without their own fault, may expect comfort under it; but when believers enter into such unions against God’s explicit warnings, they must expect distress. This caution also applies to everyday conversation. We should not form friendships and acquaintances with wicked people and unbelievers. Although we cannot completely avoid seeing and hearing and being with such people, we should never choose them as friends. We must not defile ourselves by talking with those who defile themselves with sin. Come out from among those who work evil, and separate yourselves from their empty and sinful pleasures and pursuits; from all conformity to the corruptions of this present evil world. If it is an enviable privilege to be the son or daughter of an earthly prince, how can one express the dignity and happiness of being sons and daughters of Almighty God?
